steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The question asks for the overall structure of the passage. Map out what the passage actually does: (1) introduces intermittent mass loss in asteroid 6478 Gault, (2) rules out solar energy-driven ice vaporization ('not attributable'), (3) rules out impact ejection ('untenable'), and (4) presents rotational instability as the likely correct explanation.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — It introduces a natural phenomenon, refutes two potential explanations for that phenomenon, and then presents a third explanation for that phenomenon that the author regards as plausible.: matches the passage exactly — phenomenon (mass loss), refutations (solar energy, impact ejection), third explanation (rotational instability, 'likely correct'). ✓
  • Choice B — It describes an astronomical finding, discusses competing theories about that finding that the author regards as flawed, and then describes new evidence that supports an alternative theory.: the passage never presents new evidence; it cites Luu et al.'s reasoning, not fresh data. ✗
  • Choice C — It discusses a physical process, evaluates possible causes of that process, and then states that a persuasive account of the process has yet to be put forward.: the opposite is true — the passage ends by endorsing rotational instability as likely correct. ✗
  • Choice D — It presents a scientific observation, describes a contrast between that observation and other observations, and then explains why those other observations should not be considered credible.: the passage doesn't discredit other asteroid observations — it rules out two explanations for this asteroid's mass loss. ✗
That is the correct answer.
